sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, something changed! The left green frame now has a BLACK center instead of a white center. And the right green frame (inside the dark frame) also has a black center now.
So clicking on the left green frame changed both green frames from white center to black center. This is the opposite of what happened when I moved the left green frame to the top-left corner (which changed the right green frame from black center to white center).
Let me check the segmentation to confirm.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions and colors
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")
    if n['color'] == 'c' and 10 < n['pixels'] < 50:
        print(f"Dark id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        print(f"Black id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Hmm, the left green frame still has a white center (id=5), not a black center. Let me look at the image again more carefully.
Looking at the image, the left green frame has a white center (it looks like a white square inside the green frame). The right green frame (inside the dark frame) also has a white center. So both green frames have white centers.
Wait, but earlier I thought the right green frame had a black center initially, and it changed to white center when I moved the left green frame. Now both green frames have white centers. The left green frame has always had a white center (it was the "active" one).
So the state is:
- Left green frame: rows 21-23, cols 18-20, white center
- Right green frame: rows 27-29, cols 45-47, white center, inside dark frame
The level is not completed. Maybe I need to move the left green frame inside its dark frame as well?
The left dark frame is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The interior is rows 33-35, cols 12-14.
The left green frame is at rows 21-23, cols 18-20.
To move the left green frame to the interior of the left dark frame:
- DOWN: 33 - 21 = 12 rows = 4 actions (each DOWN moves 3 rows)
- LEFT: 18 - 12 = 6 cols = 2 actions (each LEFT moves 3 cols)
But wait, the actions have been controlling the right green frame, not the left one. Let me try moving DOWN to see if the left green frame moves.
Actually, let me first check if clicking on the left green frame changed which frame is controlled. Let me try moving DOWN and see which green frame moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving DOWN to see which green frame is controlled
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
